static final class DeletingNodeVisitor extends InvalidatingNodeVisitor<InMemoryGraph> {
static final class DirtyingNodeVisitor extends InvalidatingNodeVisitor<QueryableGraph> {

*

For nodes marked dirty during invalidation ({@link DirtyType#DIRTY} and {@link
* DirtyType#CHANGE}), contains a {@link Collection} of the node's reverse deps for efficiency, so
* that the invalidator can schedule the invalidation of a node's reverse deps immediately
private final AtomicReference<Runnable> nextInvalidationCallback = new AtomicReference<>();
public static class DirtyingInvalidatorTest extends EagerInvalidatorTest {
/** The node is being looked up during the invalidation phase of Skyframe evaluation. */
private static boolean isDone(@Nullable NodeEntry entry) {
// Verify that invalidating errors causes all transient error values to be rerun.
//    invalidation on subsequent evaluations, since we don't have proper deps. See
//  - If old deserialized -> new computed, we prefer the computed value since we do have
//    proper deps and can therefore rely on the more precise classic bottom-up invalidation.
* runfiles action and output artifact must have been invalidated after the artifact corresponding
* to the lost input. Otherwise, their evaluation could race with the invalidation of the
* generating action and its output artifact. If the runfiles nodes won, they could propagate
public final void runDoneToDirtyDepForNodeInError() throws Exception {
private final AtomicInteger outputDirtyFiles = new AtomicInteger();
// TODO(bazel-team): perhaps we should come up with a separate GC class dedicated to maintaining
// Regression test: cycle node depends on error.
* This test exercises the case where we invalidate (mark dirty) a node in one build command
* invocation and the revalidation (because it did not change) happens in a subsequent build
// This query will operate over the graph as constructed by analysis, but will also pick up
// any nodes that are in the graph from prior builds, including dirty nodes. While they can't be
// reached from the scope of the current build via deps, they can appear in rdeps, which results
// in the processor doing unnecessary work or even expecting stale keys to be present. Clean
// them up now, which requires ignoring the value of --version_window_for_dirty_node_gc.
